Empires & Puzzles Rana Review: Is the Sand Queen Still Royal?

Remember when 700 attack was impressive? Neither do we. We're dusting off the original Sand Queen to see if her healing block and sand damage can survive in a world of 1600+ defense stats. Spoiler: Bring a shovel.
Rana Review: The Queen of Sand Castles (That Crumble Easily)
Rana: The Queen Who Needs a Renovation
Rana is a 5-star Holy (Yellow) hero from the
seasonal event. Originally released as a potent threat with her ability to block healing and dish out damage over time, she now stands as a relic of a simpler time. This review focuses on her

(no costume), a Monk class hero who struggles to keep up with the breakneck pace of modern Empires & Puzzles. While her costume offers a lifeline, the base form is essentially a museum piece—pretty to look at, but you probably shouldn't take it into battle.

Rana's design screams "desert royalty." Adorned in golden robes with a towering headdress and a staff that looks like it could double as a scepter, she commands authority. The background features a serene moonlit desert palace, contrasting with the harsh sandstorm she unleashes. It's a classic, high-fantasy look that fits the theme perfectly, even if her pose suggests she's patiently waiting for a stat buff that may never come.
In the current meta (early 2026), where new heroes routinely debut with 1300+ Attack and Defense and 2200+ Health
limit breaking, Rana's base power of 772 is drastically low.
This is now lower than many modern 4-star heroes. It means her direct damage will barely scratch newer tanks.
She is extremely fragile. A single sniper shot from a modern hero like
Even with full Limit Break 2 (LB2), her power only reaches 923, which is still hundreds of points behind the current standard. She is statistically obsolete.

This multiplier is incredibly low for Average speed. Against modern defenses with 1300+ defense, this will do negligible damage, likely in the double digits.
Damage over time (DoT) ignores defense, which is usually a plus. However, 141 damage per turn is easily countered by the massive boosted healing available today.
This is the crown jewel of her kit. A 75% reduction is crippling and uncleansable. It stops enemies from recovering, which is vital against heavy healers.

, making the base version significantly more selfish and less useful for team survival.

The costume is superior in every way. It changes the DoT to Water damage (higher total), increases the duration (locking down healing for longer), and crucially upgrades the cleanse to cover nearby allies. There is almost no strategic reason to use the base version if you have the costume.
Average (10 tiles). With a Level 23 mana troop (or smaller with Monk talent mana nodes), she can charge in 9 tiles.
185% of 735 attack is ~1360 raw damage value. Against a 1200 defense enemy, this results in minimal impact.
Fixed 423 damage. This is reliable but slow.
Reducing a 1000 HP heal to 250 HP is significant, effectively negating the turn for the enemy healer.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities

The Sand Empire bonus offers a Defense boost (+10%/+20%) or Healing Bonus depending on the number of heroes. While extra defense helps her fragility, it requires running other Sand Empire heroes, most of whom are similarly outdated.
Base Rana has no innate passives, a stark contrast to modern heroes who often come with three or four passive effects (mana boost on start, ailment resistance, etc.).

When Limit Broken, she gains a chance to counterattack. For a squishy hero who likely dies in one hit, this is not ideal. Defensive buffs or damage reduction would have been far better to keep her alive long enough to cast.
Upon release, Rana was a solid B+ tier hero. Her healing cut was unique and powerful for wars. She was a staple in yellow stacks for dealing with Kunchen or Telluria tanks back in the day.
The game has moved on. "Average" speed for a Hit-3 hero with sub-200% damage is unacceptable today. Modern heroes do 400-500% damage to
enemies at similar speeds, often with better secondary effects.
Base Rana is not viable in competitive play (Diamond Arena, Top 100 Wars). She may find niche use in very deep rosters for
wars purely for the healing block, or in mid-tier
This review focuses on the base hero, but the costume is the elephant in the room.
The Costume (Surfer Queen) turns her into a Cleric (Manashield is great), boosts her stats significantly, and upgrades her cleanse to a team-support skill.
owning the costume gives the base hero a stat boost (Attack +17%, Defense +17%, Health +30%, Mana +5%).

The only reason to use the base version is if you specifically need the Monk class to withstand status ailments, but even then, the Cleric's Manashield is often comparable, and the Costume's skill is strictly better.
If you are forced to use Base Rana (perhaps for a class quest), survival is your only priority.
Increases DoT slightly (DoT scales with attack).
She dies to a stiff breeze.

Don't pair her with other "Self-Cleanse" heroes if your team lacks a general cleanser. You will die to DoT.

If you pulled Rana early in your game journey, congrats! A 5-star is a 5-star. She will carry you through Map Seasons 1 and 2 easily. Her DoT is great against map bosses. Ascend her to 3/70, but think twice before using precious Darts to max her unless you have no other options.
She is food for the Soul Exchange. Unless you are a collector or have the costume and play in a very specific tiered tournament, she offers no value to a developed roster.
Back in the day, the community had mixed feelings that have only soured with time:
"I think the normal Rana needs buff, do you agree? Even the Costumed Rana needs a buff... Sand Empire is a joke compared to other seasonal events."
"Rana is underwhelming right now. She falls short compared to recent heroes."
"Rana makes a great finisher... her heal block and DoT are invaluable for me."

Rana, in her base form, is a queen without a kingdom. The shifting sands of the meta have buried her stats and skills deep beneath the dunes. While her healing block remains a unique and potent mechanic, the delivery system (the hero itself) is too fragile to be reliable. If you have her, cross your fingers for the costume—or a very generous buff from the developers.
Only usable if you have literally no other 5-star Holy heroes.
A 2026 review of Rana, the 5-star Holy hero from the Sand Empire. We analyze her stats, Sand Storm skill, and viability in the modern meta compared to her costume.
